entry.title: Ransomware Will Hit Hospitals. Rehearsals Are Key to Defense
entry.content:
A chief medical information officer describes what hospitals face when they inevitably suffer a ransomware attack—whether it leads to short- or long-term outages.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: RSAC 2026: AI Dominates, But Community Remains Key to Security
entry.content:
As AI took center stage at this year's conference, experts debated automation, oversight and the evolving role of human intelligence in cybersecurity — despite the US government's notable absence.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Security Bosses Are All-In on AI. Here's Why
entry.content:
CISOs are bullish on AI and have big plans to roll out future tools. We talk to Reddit CISO Frederick Lee and leading analyst Dave Gruber about how AI is working out in the real world, as well as its future promise.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Not Toying Around: Hasbro Attack May Take 'Weeks' to Remediate
entry.content:
The company's 8-K filing notes "unauthorized access" and that it has activated business continuity plans and taken some systems offline.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Geopolitics, AI, and Cybersecurity: Insights From RSAC 2026
entry.content:
AI-driven threats, global leadership shifts, and the future of cybersecurity in a rapidly evolving landscape were among the discussions at RSAC 2026 Conference.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Claude Source Code Leak Highlights Big Supply Chain Missteps
entry.content:
Or, why the software supply chain should be treated as critical infrastructure with guardrails built in at every layer.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Blast Radius of TeamPCP Attacks Expands Amid Hacker Infighting
entry.content:
As organizations disclose breaches tied to TeamPCP's supply chain attacks, ShinyHunters and Lapsus$ are getting involved, taking credit, and creating a murky situation for enterprises.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Cybersecurity M&A Roundup: 38 Deals Announced in March 2026
entry.content:
Significant cybersecurity M&A deals announced by Airbus, Cellebrite, Databricks, Quantum eMotion, Rapid7, and OpenAI. The post Cybersecurity M&A Roundup: 38 Deals Announced in March 2026 appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Apple Rolls Out DarkSword Exploit Protection to More Devices
entry.content:
The DarkSword exploit kit has been used by both state-sponsored hackers and commercial spyware vendors. The post Apple Rolls Out DarkSword Exploit Protection to More Devices appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Critical Vulnerability in Claude Code Emerges Days After Source Leak
entry.content:
Within days of each other, Anthropic first leaked the source code to Claude Code, and then a critical vulnerability was found by Adversa AI. The post Critical Vulnerability in Claude Code Emerges Days After Source Leak appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: North Korean Hackers Drain $285 Million From Drift in 10 Seconds
entry.content:
The attackers prepared infrastructure and multiple nonce-based transactions, took over an admin key, and drained five vaults. The post North Korean Hackers Drain $285 Million From Drift in 10 Seconds appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: T-Mobile Sets the Record Straight on Latest Data Breach Filing
entry.content:
The cybersecurity incident involved an insider and had a limited impact, the telecoms giant told SecurityWeek. The post T-Mobile Sets the Record Straight on Latest Data Breach Filing appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: React2Shell Exploited in Large-Scale Credential Harvesting Campaign
entry.content:
Using automated scanning and the Nexus Listener collection framework, the hackers compromised over 750 systems. The post React2Shell Exploited in Large-Scale Credential Harvesting Campaign appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Mobile Attack Surface Expands as Enterprises Lose Control
entry.content:
Shadow AI embedded in everyday apps, combined with outdated mobile devices and zero-click exploits, is creating a new and largely unseen mobile risk. The post Mobile Attack Surface Expands as Enterprises Lose Control appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: In Other News: ChatGPT Data Leak, Android Rootkit, Water Facility Hit by Ransomware
entry.content:
Other noteworthy stories that might have slipped under the radar: Symantec vulnerability, anti-ClickFix mechanism added to macOS, FBI hack classified as major incident. The post In Other News: ChatGPT Data Leak, Android Rootkit, Water Facility Hit by Ransomware appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: TrueConf Zero-Day Exploited in Asian Government Attacks
entry.content:
A Chinese threat actor exploited the video conferencing platform to perform reconnaissance, escalate privileges, and execute additional payloads. The post TrueConf Zero-Day Exploited in Asian Government Attacks appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Ukraine warns Russian hackers are revisiting past breaches to prepare new attacks
entry.content:
In a new report, CERT-UA said attackers are revisiting previously breached infrastructure to check whether access is still available, whether exploited vulnerabilities have been patched and whether previously obtained credentials remain valid.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Massachusetts emergency communications system impacted by cyberattack
entry.content:
An emergency communications system used across a handful of small towns in northern Massachusetts was impacted by a cyberattack.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: EU cyber agency attributes major data breach to TeamPCP hacking group
entry.content:
The European Union’s cybersecurity agency said the hacking group TeamPCP was behind a massive recent data breach at the European Commission.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: CISA gives agencies two weeks to patch video conferencing bug exploited by Chinese hackers
entry.content:
A bug in a popular line of video conferencing software is being exploited by hackers, prompting the U.S. government to order all agencies to patch the vulnerability within two weeks.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: ‘Starkiller’ Phishing Service Proxies Real Login Pages, MFA
entry.content:
Most phishing websites are little more than static copies of login pages for popular online destinations, and they are often quickly taken down by anti-abuse activists and security firms. But a stealthy new phishing-as-a-service offering lets customers sidestep both of these pitfalls: It uses cleverly disguised links to load the target brand's real website, and then acts as a relay between the target and the legitimate site -- forwarding the victim's username, password and multi-factor authentic
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: How AI Assistants are Moving the Security Goalposts
entry.content:
AI-based assistants or "agents" -- autonomous programs that have access to the user's computer, files, online services and can automate virtually any task -- are growing in popularity with developers and IT workers. But as so many eyebrow-raising headlines over the past few weeks have shown, these powerful and assertive new tools are rapidly shifting the security priorities for organizations, while blurring the lines between data and code, trusted co-worker and insider threat, ninja hacker and n
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Iran-Backed Hackers Claim Wiper Attack on Medtech Firm Stryker
entry.content:
A hacktivist group with links to Iran's intelligence agencies is claiming responsibility for a data-wiping attack against Stryker, a global medical technology company based in Michigan. News reports out of Ireland, Stryker's largest hub outside of the United States, said the company sent home more than 5,000 workers there today. Meanwhile, a voicemail message at Stryker's main U.S. headquarters says the company is currently experiencing a building emergency.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Feds Disrupt IoT Botnets Behind Huge DDoS Attacks
entry.content:
The U.S. Justice Department joined authorities in Canada and Germany in dismantling the online infrastructure behind four highly disruptive botnets that compromised more than three million hacked Internet of Things (IoT) devices, such as routers and web cameras. The feds say the four botnets -- named Aisuru, Kimwolf, JackSkid and Mossad -- are responsible for a series of recent record-smashing distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks capable of knocking nearly any target offline.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: ‘CanisterWorm’ Springs Wiper Attack Targeting Iran
entry.content:
A financially motivated data theft and extortion group is attempting to inject itself into the Iran war, unleashing a worm that spreads through poorly secured cloud services and wipes data on infected systems that use Iran's time zone or have Farsi set as the default language.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: $285 Million Drift Protocol Exploit Shows Signs of North Korea-Linked Hackers
entry.content:
Abdelaziz Fathi reports: Blockchain analytics firm Elliptic said the $285 million exploit of Solana-based Drift Protocol shows multiple indicators associated with North Korea’s state-sponsored hacking groups. The firm’s assessment is based on onchain behavior, laundering patterns, and network-level signals that align with previous incidents attributed to DPRK-linked actors. The attack is the largest crypto exploit... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Everything you need to know about the malware stealing data from Mac users
entry.content:
Matt Binder reports that Mac users have a new malware threat to be on the watch out for. According to a new report by Malwarebytes, Infiniti Stealer is a new malware attack targeting Mac users that utilizes social engineering tactics and, once the payload is delivered to the device, is very difficult to detect. The hacker’s... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: North Attleboro, Massachusetts, schools hit by suspected cyberattack
entry.content:
DysruptionHub reports: North Attleboro Public Schools in Massachusetts said Wednesday it is responding to unauthorized activity on its network after what the superintendent described as a cybersecurity incident over the past several days. The Sun Chronicle reported that Superintendent John Antonucci said the district had responded ‘over the past several days’ to unauthorized activity on its network.... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Toymaker Hasbro says it may take weeks to recover from cyberattack
entry.content:
Zack Whittaker reports: American toy-making giant Hasbro has confirmed a cyberattack, and the company says it may take “several weeks” before the incident is resolved. The owner of properties including Transformers, Peppa Pig, and Dungeons & Dragons said in a legally required disclosure with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission on Wednesday that it detected an intrusion... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Italy’s data protection regulator fined Intesa Sanpaolo €31.8 million over insider data breach
entry.content:
From the Garante’s press release, below, it sounds like the banking group experienced an insider-wrongdoing breach in which an employee improperly accessed 3,573 customer accounts over a period of two years. Data breach: The Italian Data Protection Authority fines Intesa Sanpaolo €31.8 million for unauthorized access to the banking information of over 3,500 customers for... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Apex recovers stolen personal data after about 22K impacted in cyberattack: Town
entry.content:
When criminals store or host data on U.S. servers, victims may get lucky. This is one of those situations. Matthew Sockol reports that data from the town of Apex in North Carolina had been stolen in an attempted ransomware attack in July 2024. The data of approximately 22,000 residents had reportedly never appeared on the... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Nacogdoches Memorial Hospital notifies 257,073 after January data breach
entry.content:
Chad van Alstin reports: A hospital in Texas revealed that it’s fallen victim to a data breach that exposed the personal information of more than 257,000 patients to hackers. Nacogdoches Memorial Hospital—an independent health system in Texas consisting of one emergency-capable facility, several affiliated provider practices, and a rehabilitation center—made the breach public this week.... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: European Commission investigates cyber attack on its websites; CERT-EU publishes recommendations
entry.content:
Politico reported: The European Commission is investigating a cyber attack on its websites, with early findings suggesting that some data was taken, it said Friday. The EU executive said it discovered the attack on Tuesday and took “immediate steps” to contain it. The attack hit the cloud computing infrastructure used by the Commission to manage... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: BakerHostetler’s 2026 report: Findings from 1,250 clients’ breach experiences in 2025
entry.content:
BakerHostetler’s annual report, which shares their experiences as a law firm representing data breach clients, is always one of my favorite reads, and their 2026 Data Security Incident Response Report does not disappoint. As always, it is chock-full of interesting statistics and commentary. In 2025, they represented 1,250 clients: 27% were from Healthcare (including pharma... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Questions raised after Cherry Creek students notified of data breach, lawsuit
entry.content:
Abraham Jewett reports: The Cherry Creek School District sent a message to families recently after some students received a notice about a class action settlement over a 2024 data breach involving the web-based education platform Naviance. The school district, in its message to families, clarified that the email was legitimate, and the class action lawsuit... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: FREE Webinar: Workforce HIPAA Compliance – Building Your First Line of Defense
entry.content:
Many organizations invest heavily in technology and policies, but overlook the single biggest driver of compliance risk: their workforce. This […] The post FREE Webinar: Workforce HIPAA Compliance – Building Your First Line of Defense appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Data Breach Reported by Orthopedic Implant Manufacturer TriMed
entry.content:
TriMed, a Santa Clarita, California-based manufacturer of upper and lower orthopedic implants, has announced a data security incident involving unauthorized […] The post Data Breach Reported by Orthopedic Implant Manufacturer TriMed appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Free HIPAA Compliance Risk Calculator
entry.content:
HIPAA compliance is mandatory for organizations that qualify as HIPAA covered entities. But how compliant is your organization really? With our 5-minute free HIPAA Risk Check, you can quickly evaluate the compliance status of your organization and receive a report with actionable insights to immediately improve compliance with HIPAA. The post Free HIPAA Compliance Risk Calculator appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Nacogdoches Memorial Hospital Data Breach More Than 257,000 Individuals
entry.content:
Nacogdoches Memorial Hospital (NMH), a 226-bed hospital in Nacogdoches, Texas, has recently announced a data security incident that was first […] The post Nacogdoches Memorial Hospital Data Breach More Than 257,000 Individuals appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Eye Physicians of Central Florida Data Breach Settlement
entry.content:
Eye Physicians of Central Florida has agreed to settle a class action lawsuit stemming from a 2023 data breach that […] The post Eye Physicians of Central Florida Data Breach Settlement appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Iowa AG Sues Change Healthcare Over 2024 Ransomware Attack
entry.content:
Iowa Attorney General Brenna Bird has filed a lawsuit against Change Healthcare, UnitedHealth Group, and Optum over the February 2024 […] The post Iowa AG Sues Change Healthcare Over 2024 Ransomware Attack appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Cardiovascular Consultants Pays $3.85M to Settle Data Breach Litigation
entry.content:
Cardiovascular Consultants in Arizona has settled a class action lawsuit stemming from a 2023 data breach involving the protected health […] The post Cardiovascular Consultants Pays $3.85M to Settle Data Breach Litigation appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Stryker Fully Operational After March Cyberattack
entry.content:
Stryker has announced that it has recovered systems impacted by its March 11 cyberattack and is once again fully operational […] The post Stryker Fully Operational After March Cyberattack appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Critical Cisco IMC auth bypass gives attackers Admin access
entry.content:
Cisco has patched several critical and high-severity vulnerabilities, including an Integrated Management Controller (IMC) authentication bypass that enables attackers to gain Admin access. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: Medtech giant Stryker fully operational after data-wiping attack
entry.content:
Stryker Corporation, one of the world's leading medical technology companies, says it's fully operational three weeks after many of its systems were wiped out in a cyberattack claimed by the Iranian-linked Handala hacktivist group. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: New Progress ShareFile flaws can be chained in pre-auth RCE attacks
entry.content:
Two vulnerabilities in Progress ShareFile, an enterprise-grade secure file transfer solution, can be chained to enable unauthenticated file exfiltration from affected environments. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: Drift loses $280 million as North Korean hackers seize Security Council powers
entry.content:
The Drift Protocol lost at least $280 million after a threat actor took control of its Security Council administrative powers in a planned, sophisticated operation. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: Claude Code leak used to push infostealer malware on GitHub
entry.content:
Threat actors are exploiting the recent Claude Code source code leak by using fake GitHub repositories to deliver Vidar information-stealing malware. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: CERT-EU: European Commission hack exposes data of 30 EU entities
entry.content:
The European Union's Cybersecurity Service (CERT-EU) has attributed the European Commission cloud hack to the TeamPCP threat group, saying the resulting breach exposed the data of at least 29 other Union entities. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: Evolution of Ransomware: Multi-Extortion Ransomware Attacks
entry.content:
Multi-extortion ransomware relies on stolen data to pressure victims with public leaks. Penta Security explains how its D.AMO platform keeps exfiltrated files encrypted and useless to attackers. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: Die Linke German political party confirms data stolen by Qilin ransomware
entry.content:
The Qilin ransomware group has claimed responsibility for an attack against Die Linke ('The Left'), forcing an IT systems outage at the political party, and threatening sensitive data leak. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: Hims & Hers warns of data breach after Zendesk support ticket breach
entry.content:
Telehealth giant Hims & Hers Health is warning that it suffered a data breach after support tickets were stolen from a third-party customer service platform. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.content:
Unknown reported a healthcare breach.
📰 Source: HHS OCR | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: State Road and Tollway Authority
